inline_review:
commit 830b09ea8a1f18901582f4f0d29dd07980f1cd81
Author: David Carlier <devnexen@gmail.com>

mm/page_io: use folios in sio_write_complete()

This commit converts sio_write_complete() from legacy page APIs to folio
equivalents. It replaces page-based writeback and dirty functions with their
folio counterparts and accesses the folio's swap entry directly.

> Convert sio_write_complete() from legacy page APIs to folio equivalents,
> matching the conversion already done for sio_read_complete() in commit
> 6a8c068774ad ("mm/page_io: use a folio in sio_read_complete()").

Since this conversion matches sio_read_complete(), does sio_read_complete()
undercount global PSWPIN events for large folios?

Looking at the implementation of sio_read_complete() in mm/page_io.c:

static void sio_read_complete(struct kiocb *iocb, long ret)
{
        struct swap_iocb *sio = container_of(iocb, struct swap_iocb, iocb);
        int p;

        if (ret == sio->len) {
                for (p = 0; p < sio->pages; p++) {
                        struct folio *folio = page_folio(sio->bvec[p].bv_page);
                        ...
                        count_memcg_folio_events(folio, PSWPIN, folio_nr_pages(folio));
                        ...
                }
                count_vm_events(PSWPIN, sio->pages);

The variable sio->pages tracks the number of folios (bvec entries) added to
the I/O control block, rather than the number of base pages.

When large folios are swapped in, this increments the global counter by only
1 per large folio, while the memcg counter correctly uses
folio_nr_pages(folio).

Would using sio->len >> PAGE_SHIFT instead of sio->pages correctly represent
the number of base pages processed?
